title: Kit-side context and vault-side skills must not cross over
body: This repo carries two scopes at once: kit-development context (AGENTS.md, docs/, llm_wiki_kit/) which agents working on the kit read, and vault-side skills (core/files/skills/**, templates/*/files/skills/**) which get copied into a user's vault and read by their Claude session. Never let kit-development context bleed into a vault-side SKILL.md, and never write kit code that assumes a vault path other than what's explicitly passed in. See AGENTS.md § Two scopes, one repo.

title: Never bypass write_helper.safe_write() for vault writes
body: Any kit code that writes into a user's vault must route through write_helper.safe_write(). Drift detection is load-bearing — raw open()/Path.write_text() calls escape the journal and break what wiki doctor relies on for reconciliation. If you find yourself reaching for a plain write, stop and route through the helper. See AGENTS.md § Things you should not do without asking.

title: Commit format: v2 tasks now, conventional commits later
body: During v2 development every commit corresponds to a numbered task in docs/rfc/0001-v2-architecture.md and uses the form `v2: task <N> - <one-line summary>`. After v2 ships, the repo switches to conventional commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`, `chore:`) with the affected module in parens when useful, e.g. `fix(journal): handle empty file as zero events`. See docs/CONVENTIONS.md § Commit messages.

title: Don't hand-edit CHARTER.md substantively without an RFC
body: The charter is frozen by design: substantive changes go through an RFC so scope shifts get reviewed and recorded. Trivial fixes (typos, broken links, clarifications) are fine as a normal PR, but rewriting goals, scope, or non-goals directly skips the discussion that keeps the project honest about what it is and isn't. See AGENTS.md § Things you should not do without asking and docs/CONVENTIONS.md § The doc hierarchy.
